data.title: France service activity shrinks in September
data.description:
• The seasonally adjusted HCOB France Services PMI fell to 48.5 in September, from 49.8 in August. The decrease took the survey's headline measure deeper into contraction territory, although it was indicative of a modest decline in output.
  • The HCOB France Composite PM dropped to a five month low of 48.1, after reading of 49.8 in August.
  • "French politics is currently offering little support to businesses, as the ongoing political deadlock adds to uncertainty and undermines confidence. Amidst this turmoil, France’s private sector lost ground: the HCOB France Composite PMI weakened in September, with activity declining in both the manufacturing and service sectors," said Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg at Hamburg Commercial Bank.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: OSE Immunotherapeutics replaces CEO Nicolas Poirier, appoints Marc Le Bozec as interim CEO
Spain services sector growth accelerates in September
data.description:
• Spain Services PMI Business Activity Index recorded 54.3 in September, up from 53.2 in the previous month and therefore pointed to an accelerated expansion of activity. 
  • Private sector growth was sustained in September at a solid rate. This was signalled by the HCOB Spain Composite PMI Output Index which posted 53.8 in September, little changed on August’s 53.7.
  • “The Spanish economy is skilfully navigating global challenges, staying on a growth trajectory. Compared to the previous month, Spain’s private sector slightly accelerated its pace of growth, indicated by the HCOB Composite PMI posting at 53.8 in September after 53.7 in August. Sector-wise, growth momentum in manufacturing softened somewhat, whereas the service sector gained momentum," said Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank,
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

data.title: USA Rare Earth stock jumps 10% after CEO says in talks with Trump admin - report
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ:USAR) stock jumped 10% to $24.93 on Thursday during after-market hours of trading after CEO Barbara Humpton told CNBC that the company was “in close communication” with President Trump's administration.

The after-close jump in price was followed by ~23% gains in price on Thursday during trade.

“This is a field where it will not be a zero sum game,” Humpton commented regarding the rare earth supply chain. “It’s going to take a lot of players to build out this marketplace.”

“What we’re doing is keeping the administration informed of our own plans,” she said.

This comes after Trump’s government recently secured stakes in other prominent rare earth and lithium suppliers, like MP Materials (MP) and Lithium Americas (LAC).

USAR gets a quant rating of Hold on Seeking Alpha, with the highest factor grades given to growth and momentum.

data.title: Japan's Hitachi joins hands with OpenAI over data centers - report
data.description:
Hitachi (OTCPK:HTHIY) announced a strategic partnership with OpenAI to build AI infrastructure and expand data centers globally.

The core of the collaboration is for Hitachi (OTCPK:HTHIF) to supply power transmission,cooling equipments and distribution equipment to AI data centers, Nikkei reported. 

While OpenAI will provide its large language model to Hitachi, which plans to integrate the technology into its Lumada digital platform and explore utilization strategies.

The partnership was formalized through a memorandum of understanding signed in Tokyo by Hitachi President Toshiaki Tokunaga and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. 

The Japanese conglomerate's (OTCPK:HTHIY) stock jumped about 10% on Friday on Tokyo exchange.

data.title: Google to invest $4B to build data center in Arkansas, powered by Entergy
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) said it will invest $4B in Arkansas through 2027 for the construction of a new data center, powered by Entergy (NYSE:ETR), on more than 1,000 acres in West Memphis.

Entergy Arkansas said Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) will cover the full energy costs for the facility. This will contribute over $1.1B in net benefits over the life of the contract and help reduce electricity rates for the utility's customers.

The data center will be powered by Entergy Arkansas' existing generation power portfolio and transmission grid, which will be bolstered by a new 600-megawatt solar project.

This will be backed by a 350 MW battery storage system that will be located in Jefferson County, Arkansas. Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) will pay rates that cover the cost of this resource. 

The tech giant also announced a $25M Energy Impact Fund to help scale energy efficiency and affordability initiatives for residents in Crittenden County and the surrounding area.

"The funds will go towards home weatherization, innovative energy efficiency technology and energy workforce development," Entergy (NYSE:ETR) said.

Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) will also offer Arkansas residents no-cost access to its AI courses and career certificates.

"Google's multi-billion-dollar investment in West Memphis is one of the largest investments ever in our state," said Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders.

data.title: Fiddlehead Resources CFO Ying Yuen retires; search for successor underway
Asia markets mixed as tech pullback weighs, Japan stocks rally to near record highs ahead of PM vote
data.description:
Asia stocks trade mixed on Friday due to ongoing market holidays and a general pullback in technology shares. In contrast, Japanese markets rallied sharply, climbing to near-record highs ahead of a key ruling party vote that will determine the country's next Prime Minister, and following a widely reported partnership between Hitachi and OpenAI, which boosted optimism about further artificial intelligence (AI) development in the country.

Investors also remained cautious amid concerns over the US government shutdown and calls from Democratic senators for the release of key jobs data.

U.S. President Trump said on Thursday his administration is considering issuing USD 1,000–2,000 rebate checks to American taxpayers, funded by revenue from new tariffs.

Silver slipped below $47 per ounce on Friday but remained on track for a seventh consecutive weekly gain. Gold traded around $3,860 per ounce on Friday, aiming for its seventh consecutive weekly gain after hitting a fresh high earlier this week.

Retail sales in Singapore climbed by 5.2% year-on-year in August 2025, accelerating from a downwardly revised 4.6% increase in the previous month.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 1.75% to around 45,300 and the broader Topix advanced 0.7% to 3,110 on Friday, as Japanese equities gained for a second straight session. The Japanese yen slipped to around 147.5 per dollar on Friday, retreating from two-week highs.

The S&P Global Japan Services PMI rose to 53.3 in September 2025, above the flash estimate of 53.0 and August’s 53.1. The S&P Global Japan Composite PMI registered 51.3 in September 2025, above the flash estimate of 51.1 but down from 52.1 in the previous month.

Japan’s unemployment rate rose to 2.6% in August 2025 from 2.3% in the previous month, surpassing market estimates of 2.4% and marking the highest reading since July 2024.

Separately, the Liberal Democratic Party is set to choose a successor to former Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, who resigned last month, with debates centering on household relief measures versus fiscal discipline. 

BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda reaffirmed Friday that rates will be raised if growth and inflation evolve in line with projections.

China (SHCOMP) market closed, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 7.13 per dollar on Friday, following three consecutive sessions of stability

On the evolving US-China trade relations, President Donald Trump recently indicated that he intends to urge Chinese President Xi Jinping to boost US soybean imports during their upcoming meeting, as American farmers continue to suffer from the consequences of his trade disputes. Additionally, he mentioned plans to visit China sometime next year.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.09% to 27,067 on Friday morning, ending a three-session winning streak and retreating from a four-year-high.

On the data front, Hong Kong retail sales rose 3.2% y/y in August, the strongest pace since December 2023.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.03% to 80,901 in morning trade on Friday in a choppy session, as traders booked profits after snapping an eight-session losing streak with a sharp surge in the previous session driven by weakness in auto stocks, financial services, and consumer goods.

Uncertainty surrounding US-India trade talks further weighed on sentiment.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.41% to around 8,950 on Friday, extending its 1.1% rally from the previous session, lifted by tech and healthcare stocks, and on track for its best weekly gain since mid-July. The Australian dollar edged higher to around $0.660 on Friday, recouping some losses in the prior session, and headed for its first weekly gain in three weeks.

The S&P Global Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index fell to 52.4 from 55.8 in August. The S&P Global Australia Composite PMI—which tracks both manufacturing and services—slipped to 52.4 in September from 55.5 in August.

In the U.S. on Thursday, all three major indexes ended higher, with the S&P 500 up 0.1%, the Nasdaq rising 0.4%, and the Dow adding 80 points, as the AI trade continued to drive gains amid renewed buzz around OpenAI.

U.S. stock futures edged higher on Friday after Wall Street posted fresh highs in the previous session: Dow +0.26%; S&P 500 +0.27%; Nasdaq +0.31%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Australia’s services sector expands for 20th straight month despite sharp slowdown in September

Australian trade surplus plunges to seven-year low amid tariff impact, exports tumble 7.8% m/m

China's official PMI shows slowed contraction, but private survey signals growth, creating mixed economic picture

Bank of Japan signals readiness for more rate hikes, sees tariff impact as temporary growth slowdown

data.title: Nvidia's stalled AI chip deal with UAE frustrates Huang - report
data.description:
Nvidia’s (NASDAQ:NVDA) multibillion-dollar deal to supply advanced AI chips to the United Arab Emirates has been stalled for nearly five months, frustrating CEO Jensen Huang and some senior administration officials, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter. 

The delay likely stems from U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick conditioning approval of the chip exports on the UAE making substantial U.S. investment, the report added.

Without Commerce Department approval, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) cannot deliver the chips; that approval is currently withheld until the UAE finalizes its investment commitments.

The Nvidia-UAE deal was announced during Trump’s visit to Abu Dhabi in May. 

The holdup is further complicated by U.S. national security concerns regarding the UAE’s close ties with China, raising fears that the advanced AI tech could be diverted to benefit Chinese AI ambitions.

data.title: Australia’s services sector expands for 20th straight month despite sharp slowdown in September
data.description:
The S&P Global Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index fell to 52.4 from 55.8 in August. This still signals growth, extending the sector’s expansion streak to 20 months, but represents the slowest pace seen since June.

The S&P Global Australia Composite PMI—which tracks both manufacturing and services—slipped to 52.4 in September from 55.5 in August. While this marks a full year of continuous monthly growth, the expansion was the weakest recorded since June.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.4% to around 8,950 on Friday, extending its 1.1% rally from the previous session, lifted by tech and healthcare stocks, and on track for its best weekly gain since mid-July. The Australian dollar edged higher to around $0.660 on Friday, recouping some losses in the prior session, and headed for its first weekly gain in three weeks.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWA), (NYSEARCA:FXA), (NYSEARCA:FLAU).
